rule_metadata.author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
rule_metadata.title: Unusual Module Loads - WMI

rules.rule_text:
title: Unusual Module Loads - WMI
id: b62ec77f-8014-42be-9874-c155f9ce6fae
status: experimental
description: "By monitoring and detecting on module loads, you can catch a variety\
  \ of different malicious \nactivities, including defense evasion and credential\
  \ theft. In cases where an adversary is \nusing WMI for credential theft, consider\
  \ looking for the execution of wmiprvse.exe (or its \nchild processes) with unusual\
  \ module loads like samlib.dll or vaultcli.dll. WMI is also a \nuseful vehicle for\
  \ bypassing application controls, and we commonly see adversaries\u2014real and\
  \ \nsimulated\u2013using a WMI bypass method called \u201CSquibblyTwo.\u201D The\
  \ following pseudo-detection \nanalytic is designed specifically to catch application\
  \ control bypasses, but you can likely \nadapt it to detect other threats by substituting\
  \ in a different DLL or by removing the command. \nPart of the RedCanary 2024 Threat\
  \ Detection Report.\n"
references:
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/techniques/windows-management-instrumentation/
author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
date: 2024/03/21
tags:
- attack.execution
- attack.t1047
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: \wmic.exe
    CommandLine|contains: 'format:'
    ImageLoaded|contains:
    - jscript.dll
    - vbscript.dll
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
- Unknown
level: low
